content: Epi curve interpretation requires considering: right-censoring (recent cases not yet reported), changes in testing or surveillance, and importation versus local transmission. Secondary attack rate measures person-to-person spread: cases among contacts divided by total contacts. Outbreak duration relative to incubation period distinguishes point source (<2 incubation periods) from propagated (>3 generations). Mixed patterns occur when point source triggers secondary propagation. Comparing epi curves across subpopulations reveals risk factors and transmission settings.
source: Field Epidemiology Manual (WHO), CDC training materials
